what_it_does: Declares a generated surface specification for view A3: it records the view's identity, title, status, supported platforms, represented concerns, home journey, journey membership, UI addresses, and build targets/components.
observations: Represents three concerns: NODE-config, RUN-status, ENG-force.
Home journey is J1 and journeys[] contains only J1; cross_journeys[] is empty.
Addresses are all ui:// URIs scoped to canvas, inspector, and toolbar.
build_to names components canvas, inspector, toolbar; a design_lint command; and a token-to-CSS source rule.
The _what field explains that the directory path is a journey spine and that journeys/cross_journeys carry the address web (B3).
standouts: The address list is the concrete binding surface between the J1 journey and specific UI regions.
The _what meta-comment defines directory semantics and warns 'never invent an address'.
build_to couples the spec to a lint gate and a single token-derived look source.
conventions: Uses a JSON schema with id/area/title/status/platforms/represents/home_journey/journeys/cross_journeys/addresses/build_to fields.
Addresses use a ui:// URI scheme grouped by subsystem.
Directory path encodes journey spine membership.
References external build tooling and a token file.
concerns: Status 'planned' means the spec is not yet realized in code.
cross_journeys is empty even though _what says cross_journeys carry the web (B3).
build_to.design_lint is a prose command string with an arrow and parenthetical, not a clean machine-runnable form.
build_to.tokens is a prose rule with an arrow, not a machine-readable path.
No validation mechanism for the 'never invent an address' rule is visible in the file.
No generated timestamp, version, or regeneration command is recorded.
notes: This is the planned surface spec for the A3 inspector view on the J1 journey, enumerating the UI addresses and build targets it must wire together.
questions: What do the represents values NODE-config, RUN-status, and ENG-force precisely mean?
How are the listed ui:// addresses validated against actual components?
What is the exact executable form of the design_lint command and the 'FORM gate'?
Why is cross_journeys empty if it is described as carrying the web (B3)?
Which component implements each ui:// address?
What is the relationship between A3 and B3?
How and when is this generated spec regenerated or kept in sync?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify the surface for view A3 and its address web; the file does exactly that by listing addresses, journeys, platforms, and build targets, so purpose and actual align.
apparent_themes: single source of truth for visual tokens
separation of surface contract from implementation
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a machine-readable contract between design journeys and implementation addresses, so the A3 inspector view can be built, linted, and themed from a declared spec rather than ad-hoc code.
novelty: The directory-as-journey-spine convention, the 'never invent an address' rule, and the coupling of build targets to a lint gate and a single token source make this a relatively structured design-to-code traceability artifact.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, address-level surface contract for the A3 view that links journey J1 to specific canvas, inspector, and toolbar addresses, and records build-time constraints that a fused system would keep as a traceable spec.
summary_for_embedding: A generated JSON surface specification for view A3 in area A of journey J1, listing platforms, represented concerns, UI addresses under canvas/inspector/toolbar, and build targets including a design lint gate and a token-derived CSS source.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an authoritative, build-time surface contract that maps the A3 inspector view to its UI addresses and build constraints without inventing addresses.
